title: Tool-Budget Robustness Benchmark for Agentic Research Systems

sections.heading: Problem & Motivation
context: Tool-using LLM agents (ReAct, Toolformer, AutoGPT) are evaluated almost exclusively under ideal conditions: unlimited API calls, zero latency, perfect tool output.
Real deployments face hard budget caps (e.g., $0.50/query), variable latency (50ms–30s), noisy or partial tool returns, and rate-limited APIs.
No existing benchmark systematically measures how agent performance degrades when these constraints tighten.
Without such a benchmark, practitioners cannot predict deployment cost, reliability, or failure modes before launch.
impact: Enables evidence-based tool-budget allocation for production agent systems.
Provides the first standardized degradation curves for popular agent frameworks.
Gives safety teams quantitative data on failure modes under resource pressure.
Creates a shared vocabulary and methodology for agent robustness evaluation.

sections.heading: Core Innovation
sections.content:
**Three-axis stress framework:** We propose evaluating agents along three orthogonal constraint axes — (1) tool-call budget (max calls per task), (2) latency injection (simulated network/API delays), and (3) tool-output noise (truncation, stale data, format errors). Each axis is swept independently while holding others at baseline, then combined in a full factorial grid. The harness records every tool invocation in an audit-grade trace log (OpenTelemetry-compatible JSONL), enabling post-hoc analysis of retry patterns, fallback strategies, and cascading failures.

**Key contributions:**
- A parameterized benchmark harness with pluggable agent adapters and tool simulators
- A suite of 6 task families spanning retrieval, code generation, data analysis, multi-hop QA, planning, and web navigation
- Grounded baselines: no-tool oracle, random-tool, greedy-first, and human-budget-matched
- Audit-grade JSONL trace format with OpenTelemetry span IDs for every tool call
- Pre-computed degradation curves and cost-performance Pareto frontiers for 4 agent frameworks

steps.label: TaskLoader
steps.description: Loads task instances from the 6 task families. Input: task_family + split (dev/test). Output: list[TaskInstance] with ground-truth labels.

steps.label: AgentAdapter
steps.description: Wraps target agent framework (ReAct, Toolformer, AutoGPT, custom). Input: TaskInstance + constraint config. Output: AgentTrace (tool calls, LLM calls, final answer).

steps.label: BudgetController
steps.description: Enforces tool-call budget, latency injection, and noise injection. Input: constraint_config (max_calls, latency_ms, noise_type). Output: modified tool responses or early termination.

steps.label: ToolSimulator
steps.description: Simulates external tools (search, code executor, calculator, browser). Input: tool_name + args. Output: tool_result (optionally degraded by BudgetController).

steps.label: TraceRecorder
steps.description: Records every LLM call and tool call in JSONL with OpenTelemetry span IDs. Input: agent events. Output: trace.jsonl per run.

steps.label: MetricsEngine
steps.description: Computes task accuracy, tool efficiency (calls/task), cost proxy ($), latency-adjusted throughput, and failure-mode classifier. Input: traces + ground truth. Output: metrics.json + degradation_curves.csv.

steps.label: ReportGenerator
steps.description: Produces LaTeX tables, matplotlib degradation plots, and a one-page executive summary. Input: metrics.json. Output: report/ directory with figures and tables.

items.label: TF-Retrieval
items.value: Multi-step document retrieval: find 3 supporting passages for a claim across 5 documents. 200 instances. Tools: search, read_document.

items.label: TF-CodeGen
items.value: Generate and debug Python functions to pass hidden test cases. 150 instances. Tools: code_executor, lint, test_runner.

items.label: TF-DataAnalysis
items.value: Answer statistical questions about a CSV dataset. 120 instances. Tools: pandas_query, plot_generator, calculator.

items.label: TF-MultiHopQA
items.value: Answer questions requiring 3+ reasoning hops across Wikipedia-style articles. 180 instances. Tools: search, read_page, extract_entity.

items.label: TF-Planning
items.value: Generate a valid project plan given resource constraints. 100 instances. Tools: calendar, resource_checker, dependency_solver.

items.label: TF-WebNav
items.value: Navigate simulated web pages to complete a purchase or form submission. 80 instances. Tools: click, type_text, read_page, screenshot.

items.label: NoTool Oracle
items.value: LLM answers directly without any tool access. Establishes the floor — how much does tool access actually help?

items.label: RandomTool
items.value: Agent selects tools uniformly at random until budget exhaustion. Tests whether structured selection matters.

items.label: GreedyFirst
items.value: Agent always picks the first applicable tool. Tests whether tool diversity within budget matters.

items.label: HumanBudget
items.value: Agent operates under the median human tool-call count per task family. Tests efficiency parity with humans.

items.label: UnlimitedBudget
items.value: Agent operates with no constraints (ceiling performance). Establishes the gap between ideal and constrained.

items.label: CostMatched
items.value: All agents equalized to the same dollar budget. Tests cost-normalized performance across frameworks.

items.label: Task Accuracy
items.value: Exact-match or F1 score per task family, averaged with 95% bootstrap CI.

items.label: Tool Efficiency
items.value: Mean tool calls per task, stratified by success/failure.

items.label: Cost Proxy
items.value: Estimated USD cost per task (LLM tokens × price + tool calls × simulated API cost).

items.label: Latency-Adjusted Throughput
items.value: Tasks completed per wall-clock minute under latency injection.

items.label: Failure-Mode Distribution
items.value: Classifier over 6 failure types: budget_exhaustion, timeout, wrong_tool, hallucinated_tool, cascading_error, format_error.

items.label: Degradation Slope
items.value: Linear fit slope of accuracy vs. budget reduction (steeper = more fragile).

items.label: Pareto Frontier
items.value: Cost vs. accuracy Pareto curve per agent framework.

items.label: Audit Completeness
items.value: Percentage of tool calls with full trace (span_id, timestamps, input/output hash). Target: 100%.

sections.heading: Stress Tests
sections.content:
**Budget Sweep:** Max tool calls per task swept from 1 to 50 in steps of 5. Measures accuracy degradation curve.

**Latency Injection:** Per-call latency injected at 0ms, 100ms, 500ms, 1s, 5s, 10s, 30s. Measures timeout rate and retry behavior.

**Noise Injection — Truncation:** Tool output truncated to 50%, 25%, 10% of original length. Measures information-loss tolerance.

**Noise Injection — Stale Data:** Tool returns data from 1 day, 1 week, 1 month, 1 year ago. Measures temporal sensitivity.

**Noise Injection — Format Error:** Tool returns malformed JSON, HTML entities, or encoding errors at 5%, 15%, 30%, 50% rate. Measures parser robustness.

**Combined Stress:** Full factorial of budget={5,15,50} × latency={0,500ms,5s} × noise={none,truncate-25%,format-15%}. 27 cells, 3 seeds each = 81 runs per agent per task family.

items.risk: Agent frameworks update frequently, breaking adapters
items.mitigation: Pin framework versions; use adapter pattern with version-specific subclasses; CI nightly builds.

items.risk: Simulated tools may not reflect real API behavior
items.mitigation: Validate simulator outputs against real API samples; publish simulator fidelity report.

items.risk: Full factorial grid is computationally expensive (81 runs × 6 tasks × 4 agents = 1944 runs)
items.mitigation: Use dev split (20%) for iteration; parallelize across 8 workers; cache LLM responses for deterministic replay.

items.risk: Cost proxy may not generalize across LLM pricing tiers
items.mitigation: Parameterize pricing in config; provide sensitivity analysis across 3 price points.

items.risk: Degradation curves may be non-monotonic for some agents
items.mitigation: Report raw data points alongside fitted curves; flag non-monotonic cases in the analysis.

items.risk: Publication timeline may slip if baseline integration takes longer than expected
items.mitigation: Prioritize 2 baselines (NoTool + UnlimitedBudget) for MVP; add others incrementally.

steps.label: Phase 0 — Setup & Repo (Week 1–2)
steps.description: Repository scaffold, CI/CD, dependency management, code style enforcement.

steps.label: Phase 1 — Data & Trace Schema (Week 2–4)
steps.description: Define JSONL trace schema, task instance format, build TaskLoader for 6 families.

steps.label: Phase 2 — Harness Core (Week 4–7)
steps.description: Implement BudgetController, ToolSimulator, AgentAdapter interface, TraceRecorder.

steps.label: Phase 3 — Baselines (Week 7–9)
steps.description: Integrate NoTool, RandomTool, GreedyFirst, HumanBudget, UnlimitedBudget, CostMatched.

steps.label: Phase 4 — Metrics & Reports (Week 9–11)
steps.description: MetricsEngine, degradation curve fitting, Pareto frontier computation, ReportGenerator.

steps.label: Phase 5 — Stress Tests & Packaging (Week 11–13)
steps.description: Full factorial grid execution, result analysis, PyPI packaging, documentation.

items.label: benchmark-harness
items.value: Python package (pip-installable) with CLI and programmatic API.

items.label: task-suite
items.value: 6 task families with 830 total instances (dev + test splits).

items.label: trace-schema
items.value: OpenTelemetry-compatible JSONL trace format specification.

items.label: baseline-results
items.value: Pre-computed results for 6 baselines × 6 task families.

items.label: degradation-report
items.value: LaTeX report with tables, figures, and executive summary.

items.label: agent-adapters
items.value: Adapters for ReAct, Toolformer, AutoGPT, and LangChain agents.

items.label: documentation
items.value: ReadTheDocs site with quickstart, API reference, and contribution guide.

items.label: paper-draft
items.value: Conference paper draft (8 pages + appendix) targeting NeurIPS Datasets & Benchmarks.
